reference: I am a forty-four-year-old married woman. My height is five feet three inches and my weight is sixty-six kilograms. I have multiple cysts in the uterine cervix. The larger cyst measures twelve x ten millimeters. A hypoechoic solid lesion measuring about sixteen x fourteen millimeters is seen in the body of the uterus. My uterus is bulky with a small fibroid. I also did a Pap smear test, and the impression was negative for intraepithelial lesion or malignancy. What does this mean? Am I suffering from cancer? Is there anything dangerous or serious to worry about?
text: I am a 44-year-old young woman. My height is 5 feet 3 inches, and my weight is 66 millimeters. I have much research in the uterine surface. The larger cyst measures 12 to 10 millimeters. I have a upper color lesion measuring about 16 by 14 millimeters, and 15 in the body of the uterus. My uterus is 13 with a small quadruple. I also did a pap smooth test, and my impression was negative for intraepithelial lesion from the uterus. What does this mean? Are we suffering from cancer? Is there anything dangerous or serious to learn it on?
